'''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped burial mound]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped burial mound was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The burial mound features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped burial mounds. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped burial mounds, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): - Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. - Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): - Diameter: Approx. 34 meters - Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): - Width: Approx. 24 meters - Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** * The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** * Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' - Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture burial mounds]] [[Category:History of Miyoshi City]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系}} influences. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' - Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture burial mounds]] [[Category:History of Miyoshi City]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

'''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped burial mound]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped burial mound was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The burial mound features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped burial mounds. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped burial mounds, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): - Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. - Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): - Diameter: Approx. 34 meters - Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): - Width: Approx. 24 meters - Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** * The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** * Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' - Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture burial mounds]] [[Category:History of Miyoshi City]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped burial mounds, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): - Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. - Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): - Diameter: Approx. 34 meters - Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): - Width: Approx. 24 meters - Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** * The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** * Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' - Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture burial mounds]] [[Category:History of Miyoshi City]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

'''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped burial mound]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped burial mound was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The burial mound features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped burial mounds. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped burial mounds, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): - Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. - Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): - Diameter: Approx. 34 meters - Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): - Width: Approx. 24 meters - Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** * The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** * Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' - Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture burial mounds]] [[Category:History of Miyoshi City]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

revision.text:
'''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped burial mound]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped burial mound was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The burial mound features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped burial mounds. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped burial mounds, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): - Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. - Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): - Diameter: Approx. 34 meters - Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): - Width: Approx. 24 meters - Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** * The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** * Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' - Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture burial mounds]] [[Category:History of Miyoshi City]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

'''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped burial mound]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped burial mound was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The burial mound features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped burial mounds. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped burial mounds, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): - Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. - Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): - Diameter: Approx. 34 meters - Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): - Width: Approx. 24 meters - Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** * The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** * Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' - Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture burial mounds]] [[Category:History of Miyoshi City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' - Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture burial mounds]] [[Category:History of Miyoshi City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

revision.text:
'''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped burial mound]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped burial mound was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The burial mound features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped burial mounds. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped burial mounds, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): - Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. - Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): - Diameter: Approx. 34 meters - Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): - Width: Approx. 24 meters - Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** * The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** * Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' - Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture burial mounds]] [[Category:History of Miyoshi City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January[[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped burial mounds, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): - Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. - Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): - Diameter: Approx. 34 meters - Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): - Width: Approx. 24 meters - Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** * The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** * Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' - Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture burial mounds]] [[Category:History of Miyoshi City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January[[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

'''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped Kofun]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped Kofun was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The Kofun features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped Kofuns. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): - Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. - Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): - Diameter: Approx. 34 meters - Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): - Width: Approx. 24 meters - Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** * The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** * Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹}}. Kofun]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:%E9%85%92%E5%B1%8B%E9%AB%98%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

'''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped Kofun]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped Kofun was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The Kofun features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped Kofuns. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): - Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. - Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): - Diameter: Approx. 34 meters - Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): - Width: Approx. 24 meters - Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** * The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** * Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' - Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture Kofuns]] [[Category:History of Miyoshi City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January[[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

'''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped Kofun]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped Kofun was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The Kofun features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped Kofuns. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): - Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. - Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): - Diameter: Approx. 34 meters - Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): - Width: Approx. 24 meters - Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** * The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** * Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' - Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture Kofuns]] [[Category:History of Miyoshi City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January[[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' - Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture Kofuns]] [[Category:History of Miyoshi City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January[[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

'''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped Kofun]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped Kofun was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The Kofun features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped Kofuns. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): - Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. - Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): - Diameter: Approx. 34 meters - Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): - Width: Approx. 24 meters - Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** ** The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' - Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture Kofuns]] [[Category:History of Miyoshi City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January[[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): - Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. - Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): - Diameter: Approx. 34 meters - Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): - Width: Approx. 24 meters - Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** ** The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' - Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture Kofuns]] [[Category:History of Miyoshi City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:01-17]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January[[Category:completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]
