'''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped Kofun]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped Kofun was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The Kofun features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped Kofuns. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): - Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. - Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): - Diameter: Approx. 34 meters - Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): - Width: Approx. 24 meters - Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** ** The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹}}. '''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped Kofun]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped Kofun was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The Kofun features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped Kofuns. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): ** Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. ** Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): ** Diameter: Approx. 34 meters ** Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): ** Width: Approx. 24 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** ** The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ** Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture Kofuns]] [[Category:History of Miyoshi City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January[[Category:completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

'''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped Kofun]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped Kofun was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The Kofun features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped Kofuns. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): ** Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. ** Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): ** Diameter: Approx. 34 meters ** Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): ** Width: Approx. 24 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=draft}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|12=draft}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** ** The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|12=draft}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学|12=draft}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=draft}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系|12=draft}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹|12=draft}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ** Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=draft}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)|12=draft}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

'''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped Kofun]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped Kofun was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The Kofun features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped Kofuns. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): ** Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. ** Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): ** Diameter: Approx. 34 meters ** Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): ** Width: Approx. 24 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=draft}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|12=draft}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** ** The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|12=draft}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学|12=draft}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権|12=draft}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=draft}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系|12=draft}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹|12=draft}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ** Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=draft}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)|12=draft}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]
{{nihongo2|[[:ja:酒屋高塚古墳|酒屋高塚古墳]]}}
''Sakayatakatsuka kofun'' || [[Miyoshi, Hiroshima|Miy ... 71 KB (8,196 words) - 18:55, 10 January {{draft categories| [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture Kofuns]] [[Category:History of Miyoshi City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] [[sn:akaya Takatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:%E9%85%92%E5%B1%8B%E9%AB%98%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

{{混同|粟屋高塚古墳|x1=広島県三次市粟屋町にある}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 酒屋高塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
残存墳丘（後円部） |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[広島県]][[三次市]]西酒屋町1035-1・2 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 47|緯度秒 = 26.65 |経度度 =132|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 59.15 |ISO = JP-34 |形状 = [[帆立貝形古墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長46m
高さ7m |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]]2基 |出土品 = 銅鏡ほか副葬品多数 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 広島県指定史跡「酒屋高塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Hiroshima |アイコン = 帆立貝形古墳-西 |ラベル = 酒屋
高塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = right }} '''酒屋高塚古墳'''（さけやたかつかこふん）は、[[広島県]][[三次市]]西酒屋町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 広島県北部、[[三次盆地]]南縁の丘陵先端部に築造された大型帆立貝形古墳である{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}。墳丘は大きく削平を受け、[[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）には盗掘に遭っているほか{{Sfn|広島県の地名|1982}}、[[1982年]]（昭和57年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は、前方部（方形部）が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形で、方形部を西方向に向ける。円丘部（後円部）の墳丘は2段築成。墳丘外表では鉢巻状の[[葺石]]のほか、[[円筒埴輪]]（朝顔形埴輪含む）が認められるが、特に埴輪の表面に吉備地方特有の押圧技法が認められる点で特色を示す。埋葬施設は円丘部における[[竪穴式石室]]2基（第1号主体・第2号主体）で、石室構造には渡来系要素が指摘される。第1号主体は1941年の盗掘の際に大きく破壊を受けているが、その際に画文帯神獣鏡・鉄製品などの副葬品が出土しており、第2号主体では調査の際に鉄製品や勾玉・ガラス小玉などが出土している。副葬品のうちでは、全国で20数面の出土例がある画文帯神獣鏡、渡来系要素を持つ鉄釘が注目される。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}。広島県には帆立貝形古墳が多く、特に三次地域はその集中的な分布地域として知られており、畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との関係性を考察するうえで注目される『霧に包まれた古墳の謎 -大王の時代と三次盆地-』平成30年度秋の特別企画展展示図録、 広島県立歴史民俗資料館、2018年、pp. 19-31。。特に本古墳の場合には、画文帯神獣鏡・石室（および鉄釘）・埴輪にそれぞれ畿内ヤマト王権・朝鮮半島・吉備地方との交流が示唆されており、当時の様相を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は1982年（昭和57年）に広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）、後円部竪穴式石室の盗掘。副葬品出土（現在は[[京都大学]]所蔵）{{Sfn|広島県の地名|1982}}。 * [[1982年]]（昭和57年） ** 発掘調査（広島県教育委員会、[[1983年]]に報告書刊行）。 ** 10月14日、広島県指定史跡に指定。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：約46メートル * 円丘部（後円部） ** 直径：約34メートル ** 高さ：7メートル * 方形部（前方部） ** 幅：約24メートル ** 高さ：2.5メートル == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|移築第2号主体}}{{small|[[広島県立みよし風土記の丘]]展示。}}]] [[ファイル:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|[[広島県立みよし風土記の丘|みよし風土記の丘ミュージアム]]展示。}}]] 埋葬施設として、円丘部において[[竪穴式石室]]2基（第1号主体・第2号主体）が構築されている。それぞれの詳細は次の通り。 * 第1号主体（第1号石室） *: 1941年（昭和16年）に盗掘に遭い大半が破壊されている{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}。石室は長さ3.6メートル（推定）・幅0.83メートル・高さ1.2メートルを測る。出土した副葬品としては画文帯神獣鏡、鉄鏃、鉄斧、鉄鍬、鉄刀子、鉄釘などがある（現在は[[京都大学]]所蔵）。特に画文帯神獣鏡は舶載の中国製鏡とされるが、同じ鏡は全国の古墳で20数面が出土しており、畿内ヤマト王権からの配布を示す資料とされる。 * 第2号主体（第2号石室） *: 1982年（昭和57年）の調査の際に発見され{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}、現在は[[広島県立みよし風土記の丘]]内に移築・復原されている。石室は長さ2.72メートル・幅0.7-0.84メートル・高さ0.5メートルを測る。出土した副葬品としては鉄剣、鉄釘、勾玉、ガラス小玉などがある。 以上の石室2基には石材・粘土の使い方の点で渡来系要素が指摘される。また鉄釘は木棺に使用されたものであるが、鎹と同様に元は日本列島にはなく朝鮮半島に系譜を追う資料とされる。 Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|移築第2号主体俯瞰図 Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|移築第2号主体展開図 == 文化財 == === 広島県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 酒屋高塚古墳 - 1982年（昭和57年）10月14日指定（指定範囲面積は2,159平方メートル）[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120960.html 酒屋高塚古墳]（広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」）。史跡説明板。。 == 関連施設 == * [[広島県立みよし風土記の丘]]（[[三次市]]小田幸町） - 酒屋高塚古墳の第2号石室を移築・復原。 * [[広島県立みよし風土記の丘|みよし風土記の丘ミュージアム（広島県立歴史民俗資料館）]]（三次市小田幸町） - 酒屋高塚古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（三次市教育委員会、2012年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=高塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=高塚古墳 > 酒屋高塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/46724 酒屋高塚古墳]|publisher=広島県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2004|chapter=酒屋高塚古墳|title=三次市史|volume=II|publisher=三次市|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun}} {{デフォルトソート:さけやたかつかこふん}} [[Category:広島県の古墳]] [[Category:三次市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:広島県指定史跡]]

{{混同|粟屋高塚古墳|x1=広島県三次市粟屋町にある}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 酒屋高塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
残存墳丘（後円部） |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[広島県]][[三次市]]西酒屋町1035-1・2 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 47|緯度秒 = 26.65 |経度度 =132|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 59.15 |ISO = JP-34 |形状 = [[帆立貝形古墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長46m
高さ7m |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]]2基 |出土品 = 銅鏡ほか副葬品多数 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 広島県指定史跡「酒屋高塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Hiroshima |アイコン = 帆立貝形古墳-西 |ラベル = 酒屋
高塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = right }} '''酒屋高塚古墳'''（さけやたかつかこふん）は、[[広島県]][[三次市]]西酒屋町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 広島県北部、[[三次盆地]]南縁の丘陵先端部に築造された大型帆立貝形古墳である{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}。墳丘は大きく削平を受け、[[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）に盗掘に遭っているほか{{Sfn|広島県の地名|1982}}、[[1982年]]（昭和57年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は、前方部（方形部）が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形で、方形部を西方向に向ける。円丘部（後円部）の墳丘は2段築成。墳丘外表では鉢巻状の[[葺石]]のほか、[[円筒埴輪]]（朝顔形埴輪含む）が認められるが、特に埴輪の表面に吉備地方特有の押圧技法が認められる点で特色を示す。埋葬施設は円丘部における[[竪穴式石室]]2基（第1号主体・第2号主体）で、石室構造には渡来系要素が指摘される。第1号主体は1941年の盗掘の際に大きく破壊を受けているが、その際に画文帯神獣鏡・鉄製品などの副葬品が出土しており、第2号主体では調査の際に鉄製品や勾玉・ガラス小玉などが出土している。副葬品のうちでは、全国で20数面の出土例がある画文帯神獣鏡、渡来系要素を持つ鉄釘が注目される。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}。広島県には帆立貝形古墳が多く、特に三次地域はその集中的な分布地域として知られており、畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との関係性を考察するうえで注目される『霧に包まれた古墳の謎 -大王の時代と三次盆地-』平成30年度秋の特別企画展展示図録、 広島県立歴史民俗資料館、2018年、pp. 19-31。。特に本古墳の場合には、画文帯神獣鏡・石室（および鉄釘）・埴輪にそれぞれ畿内ヤマト王権・朝鮮半島・吉備地方との交流が示唆されており、当時の様相を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は1982年（昭和57年）に広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）、後円部竪穴式石室の盗掘。副葬品出土（現在は[[京都大学]]所蔵）{{Sfn|広島県の地名|1982}}。 * [[1982年]]（昭和57年） ** 発掘調査（広島県教育委員会、[[1983年]]に報告）。 ** 10月14日、広島県指定史跡に指定。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：約46メートル * 円丘部（後円部） ** 直径：約34メートル ** 高さ：7メートル * 方形部（前方部） ** 幅：約24メートル ** 高さ：2.5メートル == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|移築第2号主体}}{{small|[[広島県立みよし風土記の丘]]展示。}}]] [[ファイル:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|[[広島県立みよし風土記の丘|みよし風土記の丘ミュージアム]]展示。}}]] 埋葬施設として、円丘部において[[竪穴式石室]]2基（第1号主体・第2号主体）が構築されている。それぞれの詳細は次の通り。 * 第1号主体（第1号石室） *: 1941年（昭和16年）に盗掘に遭い大半が破壊されている{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}。石室は長さ3.6メートル（推定）・幅0.83メートル・高さ1.2メートルを測る。出土した副葬品としては画文帯神獣鏡、鉄鏃、鉄斧、鉄鍬、鉄刀子、鉄釘などがある（現在は[[京都大学]]所蔵）。特に画文帯神獣鏡は舶載の中国製鏡とされるが、同じ鏡は全国の古墳で20数面が出土しており、畿内ヤマト王権からの配布を示す資料とされる。 * 第2号主体（第2号石室） *: 1982年（昭和57年）の調査の際に発見され{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}、現在は[[広島県立みよし風土記の丘]]内に移築・復元されている。石室は長さ2.72メートル・幅0.7-0.84メートル・高さ0.5メートルを測る。出土した副葬品としては鉄剣、鉄釘、勾玉、ガラス小玉などがある。 以上の石室2基には石材・粘土の使い方の点で渡来系要素が指摘される。また鉄釘は木棺に使用されたものであるが、鎹と同様に元は日本列島にはなく朝鮮半島に系譜を追う資料とされる。 Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|移築第2号主体俯瞰図 Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|移築第2号主体展開図 == 文化財 == === 広島県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 酒屋高塚古墳 - 1982年（昭和57年）10月14日指定（指定範囲面積は2,159平方メートル）[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120960.html 酒屋高塚古墳]（広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」）。史跡説明板。。 == 関連施設 == * [[広島県立みよし風土記の丘]]（[[三次市]]小田幸町） - 酒屋高塚古墳の第2号石室を移築・復元。 * [[広島県立みよし風土記の丘|みよし風土記の丘ミュージアム（広島県立歴史民俗資料館）]]（三次市小田幸町） - 酒屋高塚古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（三次市教育委員会、2012年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=高塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=高塚古墳 > 酒屋高塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/46724 酒屋高塚古墳]|publisher=広島県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国文化財総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2004|chapter=酒屋高塚古墳|title=三次市史|volume=II|publisher=三次市|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun}} {{デフォルトソート:さけやたかつかこふん}} [[Category:広島県の古墳]] [[Category:三次市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:広島県指定史跡]]

高塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = right }} '''酒屋高塚古墳'''（さけやたかつかこふん）は、[[広島県]][[三次市]]西酒屋町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 広島県北部、[[三次盆地]]南縁の丘陵先端部に築造された大型帆立貝形古墳である{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}。墳丘は大きく削平を受け、[[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）に盗掘に遭っているほか{{Sfn|広島県の地名|1982}}、[[1982年]]（昭和57年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は、前方部（方形部）が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形で、方形部を西方向に向ける。円丘部（後円部）の墳丘は2段築成。墳丘外表では鉢巻状の[[葺石]]のほか、[[円筒埴輪]]（朝顔形埴輪含む）が認められるが、特に埴輪の表面に吉備地方特有の押圧技法が認められる点で特色を示す。埋葬施設は円丘部における[[竪穴式石室]]2基（第1号主体・第2号主体）で、石室構造には渡来系要素が指摘される。第1号主体は1941年の盗掘の際に大きく破壊を受けているが、その際に画文帯神獣鏡・鉄製品などの副葬品が出土しており、第2号主体では調査の際に鉄製品や勾玉・ガラス小玉などが出土している。副葬品のうちでは、全国で20数面の出土例がある画文帯神獣鏡、渡来系要素を持つ鉄釘が注目される。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}。広島県には帆立貝形古墳が多く、特に三次地域はその集中的な分布地域として知られており、畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との関係性を考察するうえで注目される『霧に包まれた古墳の謎 -大王の時代と三次盆地-』平成30年度秋の特別企画展展示図録、 広島県立歴史民俗資料館、2018年、pp. 19-31。。特に本古墳の場合には、画文帯神獣鏡・石室（および鉄釘）・埴輪にそれぞれ畿内ヤマト王権・朝鮮半島・吉備地方との交流が示唆されており、当時の様相を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は1982年（昭和57年）に広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）、後円部竪穴式石室の盗掘。副葬品出土（現在は[[京都大学]]所蔵）{{Sfn|広島県の地名|1982}}。 * [[1982年]]（昭和57年） ** 発掘調査（広島県教育委員会、[[1983年]]に報告）。 ** 10月14日、広島県指定史跡に指定。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：約46メートル * 円丘部（後円部） ** 直径：約34メートル ** 高さ：7メートル * 方形部（前方部） ** 幅：約24メートル ** 高さ：2.5メートル == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|移築第2号主体}}{{small|[[広島県立みよし風土記の丘]]展示。}}]] [[ファイル:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|[[広島県立みよし風土記の丘|みよし風土記の丘ミュージアム]]展示。}}]] 埋葬施設として、円丘部において[[竪穴式石室]]2基（第1号主体・第2号主体）が構築されている。それぞれの詳細は次の通り。 * 第1号主体（第1号石室） *: 1941年（昭和16年）に盗掘に遭い大半が破壊されている{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}。石室は長さ3.6メートル（推定）・幅0.83メートル・高さ1.2メートルを測る。出土した副葬品としては画文帯神獣鏡、鉄鏃、鉄斧、鉄鍬、鉄刀子、鉄釘などがある（現在は[[京都大学]]所蔵）。特に画文帯神獣鏡は舶載の中国製鏡とされるが、同じ鏡は全国の古墳で20数面が出土しており、畿内ヤマト王権からの配布を示す資料とされる。 * 第2号主体（第2号石室） *: 1982年（昭和57年）の調査の際に発見され{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}、現在は[[広島県立みよし風土記の丘]]内に移築・復元されている。石室は長さ2.72メートル・幅0.7-0.84メートル・高さ0.5メートルを測る。出土した副葬品としては鉄剣、鉄釘、勾玉、ガラス小玉などがある。 以上の石室2基には石材・粘土の使い方の点で渡来系要素が指摘される。また鉄釘は木棺に使用されたものであるが、鎹と同様に元は日本列島にはなく朝鮮半島に系譜を追う資料とされる。 Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|移築第2号主体俯瞰図 Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|移築第2号主体展開図 == 文化財 == === 広島県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 酒屋高塚古墳 - 1982年（昭和57年）10月14日指定（指定範囲面積は2,159平方メートル）[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120960.html 酒屋高塚古墳]（広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」）。史跡説明板。。 == 関連施設 == * [[広島県立みよし風土記の丘]]（[[三次市]]小田幸町） - 酒屋高塚古墳の第2号石室を移築・復元。 * [[広島県立みよし風土記の丘|みよし風土記の丘ミュージアム（広島県立歴史民俗資料館）]]（三次市小田幸町） - 酒屋高塚古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（三次市教育委員会、2012年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=高塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=高塚古墳 > 酒屋高塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/46724 酒屋高塚古墳]|publisher=広島県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国文化財総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2004|chapter=酒屋高塚古墳|title=三次市史|volume=II|publisher=三次市|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun}} {{デフォルトソート:さけやたかつかこふん}} [[Category:広島県の古墳]] [[Category:三次市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:広島県指定史跡]]

{{混同|粟屋高塚古墳|x1=広島県三次市粟屋町にある}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 酒屋高塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|竪穴式石室|" to "|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|"
revision.text:
'''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped Kofun]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped Kofun was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The Kofun features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped Kofuns. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): ** Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. ** Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): ** Diameter: Approx. 34 meters ** Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): ** Width: Approx. 24 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** ** The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学|12=draft|12=draft}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権|12=draft|12=draft}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=draft|12=draft}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系|12=draft|12=draft}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹|12=draft|12=draft}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ** Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=draft|12=draft}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]
{{nihongo2|[[:ja:酒屋高塚古墳|酒屋高塚古墳]]}}
''Sakayatakatsuka kofun'' || [[Miyoshi, Hiroshima|Miy ... 71 KB (8,196 words) - 18:55, 10 January {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture Kofuns]] [[Category:History of Miyoshi City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] [[sn:akaya Takatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:%E9%85%92%E5%B1%8B%E9%AB%98%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

'''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped Kofun]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped Kofun was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The Kofun features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped Kofuns. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): ** Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. ** Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): ** Diameter: Approx. 34 meters ** Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): ** Width: Approx. 24 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** ** The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ** Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]
{{nihongo2|[[:ja:酒屋高塚古墳|酒屋高塚古墳]]}}

revision.comment: 21 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:酒屋高塚古墳 up to rev 104540834
revision.text:
{{混同|粟屋高塚古墳|x1=広島県三次市粟屋町にある}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 酒屋高塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
残存墳丘（後円部） |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[広島県]][[三次市]]西酒屋町1035-1・2 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 47|緯度秒 = 26.65 |経度度 =132|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 59.15 |ISO = JP-34 |形状 = [[帆立貝形古墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長46m
高さ7m |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]]2基 |出土品 = 銅鏡ほか副葬品多数 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 広島県指定史跡「酒屋高塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Hiroshima |アイコン = 帆立貝形古墳-西 |ラベル = 酒屋
高塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = right }} '''酒屋高塚古墳'''（さけやたかつかこふん）は、[[広島県]][[三次市]]西酒屋町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 広島県北部、[[三次盆地]]南縁の丘陵先端部に築造された大型帆立貝形古墳である{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}。墳丘は大きく削平を受け、[[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）に盗掘に遭っているほか{{Sfn|広島県の地名|1982}}、[[1982年]]（昭和57年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は、前方部（方形部）が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形で、方形部を西方向に向ける。円丘部（後円部）の墳丘は2段築成。墳丘外表では鉢巻状の[[葺石]]のほか、[[円筒埴輪]]（朝顔形埴輪含む）が認められるが、特に埴輪の表面に吉備地方特有の押圧技法が認められる点で特色を示す。埋葬施設は円丘部における[[竪穴式石室]]2基（第1号主体・第2号主体）で、石室構造には渡来系要素が指摘される。第1号主体は1941年の盗掘の際に大きく破壊を受けているが、その際に画文帯神獣鏡・鉄製品などの副葬品が出土しており、第2号主体では調査の際に鉄製品や勾玉・ガラス小玉などが出土している。副葬品のうちでは、全国で20数面の出土例がある画文帯神獣鏡、渡来系要素を持つ鉄釘が注目される。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}。広島県には帆立貝形古墳が多く、特に三次地域はその集中的な分布地域として知られており、畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との関係性を考察するうえで注目される『霧に包まれた古墳の謎 -大王の時代と三次盆地-』平成30年度秋の特別企画展展示図録、 広島県立歴史民俗資料館、2018年、pp. 19-31。。特に本古墳の場合には、画文帯神獣鏡・石室（および鉄釘）・埴輪にそれぞれ畿内ヤマト王権・朝鮮半島・吉備地方との交流が示唆されており、当時の様相を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は1982年（昭和57年）に広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）、後円部竪穴式石室の盗掘。副葬品出土（現在は[[京都大学]]所蔵）{{Sfn|広島県の地名|1982}}。 * [[1982年]]（昭和57年） ** 発掘調査（広島県教育委員会、[[1983年]]に報告）。 ** 10月14日、広島県指定史跡に指定。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：約46メートル * 円丘部（後円部） ** 直径：約34メートル ** 高さ：7メートル * 方形部（前方部） ** 幅：約24メートル ** 高さ：2.5メートル == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|移築第2号主体}}{{small|[[広島県立みよし風土記の丘]]展示。}}]] [[ファイル:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|[[広島県立みよし風土記の丘|みよし風土記の丘ミュージアム]]展示。}}]] 埋葬施設として、円丘部において[[竪穴式石室]]2基（第1号主体・第2号主体）が構築されている。それぞれの詳細は次の通り。 * 第1号主体（第1号石室） *: 1941年（昭和16年）に盗掘に遭い大半が破壊されている{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}。石室は長さ3.6メートル（推定）・幅0.83メートル・高さ1.2メートルを測る。出土した副葬品としては画文帯神獣鏡、鉄鏃、鉄斧、鉄鍬、鉄刀子、鉄釘などがある（現在は[[京都大学]]所蔵）。特に画文帯神獣鏡は舶載の中国製鏡とされるが、同じ鏡は全国の古墳で20数面が出土しており、畿内ヤマト王権からの配布を示す資料とされる。 * 第2号主体（第2号石室） *: 1982年（昭和57年）の調査の際に発見され{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}、現在は[[広島県立みよし風土記の丘]]内に移築・復元されている。石室は長さ2.72メートル・幅0.7-0.84メートル・高さ0.5メートルを測る。出土した副葬品としては鉄剣、鉄釘、勾玉、ガラス小玉などがある。 以上の石室2基には石材・粘土の使い方の点で渡来系要素が指摘される。また鉄釘は木棺に使用されたものであるが、鎹と同様に元は日本列島にはなく朝鮮半島に系譜を追う資料とされる。 Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|移築第2号主体俯瞰図 Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|移築第2号主体展開図 == 文化財 == === 広島県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 酒屋高塚古墳 - 1982年（昭和57年）10月14日指定（指定範囲面積は2,159平方メートル）[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120960.html 酒屋高塚古墳]（広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」）。史跡説明板。。 == 関連施設 == * [[広島県立みよし風土記の丘]]（[[三次市]]小田幸町） - 酒屋高塚古墳の第2号石室を移築・復元。 * [[広島県立みよし風土記の丘|みよし風土記の丘ミュージアム（広島県立歴史民俗資料館）]]（三次市小田幸町） - 酒屋高塚古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（三次市教育委員会、2012年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=高塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=高塚古墳 > 酒屋高塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/46724 酒屋高塚古墳]|publisher=広島県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国文化財総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2004|chapter=酒屋高塚古墳|title=三次市史|volume=II|publisher=三次市|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun}} {{デフォルトソート:さけやたかつかこふん}} [[Category:広島県の古墳]] [[Category:三次市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:広島県指定史跡]]

墳形は、前方部（方形部）が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形で、方形部を西方向に向ける。円丘部（後円部）の墳丘は2段築成。墳丘外表では鉢巻状の[[葺石]]のほか、[[円筒埴輪]]（朝顔形埴輪含む）が認められるが、特に埴輪の表面に吉備地方特有の押圧技法が認められる点で特色を示す。埋葬施設は円丘部における[[竪穴式石室]]2基（第1号主体・第2号主体）で、石室構造には渡来系要素が指摘される。第1号主体は1941年の盗掘の際に大きく破壊を受けているが、その際に画文帯神獣鏡・鉄製品などの副葬品が出土しており、第2号主体では調査の際に鉄製品や勾玉・ガラス小玉などが出土している。副葬品のうちでは、全国で20数面の出土例がある画文帯神獣鏡、渡来系要素を持つ鉄釘が注目される。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}。広島県には帆立貝形古墳が多く、特に三次地域はその集中的な分布地域として知られており、畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との関係性を考察するうえで注目される『霧に包まれた古墳の謎 -大王の時代と三次盆地-』平成30年度秋の特別企画展展示図録、 広島県立歴史民俗資料館、2018年、pp. 19-31。。特に本古墳の場合には、画文帯神獣鏡・石室（および鉄釘）・埴輪にそれぞれ畿内ヤマト王権・朝鮮半島・吉備地方との交流が示唆されており、当時の様相を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は1982年（昭和57年）に広島県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1941年]]（[[昭和]]16年）、後円部竪穴式石室の盗掘。副葬品出土（現在は[[京都大学]]所蔵）{{Sfn|広島県の地名|1982}}。 * [[1982年]]（昭和57年） ** 発掘調査（広島県教育委員会、[[1983年]]に報告）。 ** 10月14日、広島県指定史跡に指定。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：約46メートル * 円丘部（後円部） ** 直径：約34メートル ** 高さ：7メートル * 方形部（前方部） ** 幅：約24メートル ** 高さ：2.5メートル == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|移築第2号主体}}{{small|[[広島県立みよし風土記の丘]]展示。}}]] [[ファイル:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|[[広島県立みよし風土記の丘|みよし風土記の丘ミュージアム]]展示。}}]] 埋葬施設として、円丘部において[[竪穴式石室]]2基（第1号主体・第2号主体）が構築されている。それぞれの詳細は次の通り。 * 第1号主体（第1号石室） *: 1941年（昭和16年）に盗掘に遭い大半が破壊されている{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}。石室は長さ3.6メートル（推定）・幅0.83メートル・高さ1.2メートルを測る。出土した副葬品としては画文帯神獣鏡、鉄鏃、鉄斧、鉄鍬、鉄刀子、鉄釘などがある（現在は[[京都大学]]所蔵）。特に画文帯神獣鏡は舶載の中国製鏡とされるが、同じ鏡は全国の古墳で20数面が出土しており、畿内ヤマト王権からの配布を示す資料とされる。 * 第2号主体（第2号石室） *: 1982年（昭和57年）の調査の際に発見され{{Sfn|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}、現在は[[広島県立みよし風土記の丘]]内に移築・復元されている。石室は長さ2.72メートル・幅0.7-0.84メートル・高さ0.5メートルを測る。出土した副葬品としては鉄剣、鉄釘、勾玉、ガラス小玉などがある。 以上の石室2基には石材・粘土の使い方の点で渡来系要素が指摘される。また鉄釘は木棺に使用されたものであるが、鎹と同様に元は日本列島にはなく朝鮮半島に系譜を追う資料とされる。 Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|移築第2号主体俯瞰図 Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|移築第2号主体展開図 == 文化財 == === 広島県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 酒屋高塚古墳 - 1982年（昭和57年）10月14日指定（指定範囲面積は2,159平方メートル）[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120960.html 酒屋高塚古墳]（広島県教育委員会「ホットライン教育ひろしま」）。史跡説明板。。 == 関連施設 == * [[広島県立みよし風土記の丘]]（[[三次市]]小田幸町） - 酒屋高塚古墳の第2号石室を移築・復元。 * [[広島県立みよし風土記の丘|みよし風土記の丘ミュージアム（広島県立歴史民俗資料館）]]（三次市小田幸町） - 酒屋高塚古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（三次市教育委員会、2012年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=高塚古墳|title=広島県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]35|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|広島県の地名|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小都隆|year=1989|chapter=高塚古墳 > 酒屋高塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|日本古墳大辞典|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/46724 酒屋高塚古墳]|publisher=広島県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国文化財総覧」。 * {{Cite '''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped Kofun]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped Kofun was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The Kofun features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped Kofuns. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): ** Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. ** Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): ** Diameter: Approx. 34 meters ** Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): ** Width: Approx. 24 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** ** The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ** Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ** Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

'''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped Kofun]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped Kofun was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The Kofun features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped Kofuns. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): ** Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. ** Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): ** Diameter: Approx. 34 meters ** Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): ** Width: Approx. 24 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ** Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Miyoshi Fudoki Hill]] → [[Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill]]
revision.text:
'''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped Kofun]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped Kofun was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The Kofun features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped Kofuns. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[Kyoto University]]). * [[1982]] (Shōwa 57): ** Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. ** Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): ** Diameter: Approx. 34 meters ** Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): ** Width: Approx. 24 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** ** The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|qq=}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at {{ill|Kyoto University|ja|京都大学|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto University|qq=}}). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraijin|qq=}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other {{ill|Staple (fastener)|lt=kasugai nails|ja|鎹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Staple (fastener)|qq=}}. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ** Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]
{{nihongo2|[[:ja:酒屋高塚古墳|酒屋高塚古墳]]}}
''Sakayatakatsuka kofun'' || [[Miyoshi, Hiroshima|Miy ... 71 KB (8,196 words) - 18:55, 10 January {{translated page|ja|酒屋高塚古墳|version=104540834|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture Kofuns]] [[Category:History of Miyoshi City]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] }} [[sn:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] [[sn:akaya Takatsuka Kofun]] [[ja:%E9%85%92%E5%B1%8B%E9%AB%98%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]]

Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ** Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun|Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]
