The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[:en:1941|1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]). * [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57): ** Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. ** Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): ** Diameter: Approx. 34 meters ** Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): ** Width: Approx. 24 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|lt=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|lt=Pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** ** The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|lt=wide image-band deity and beast mirror|1=Shinju-kyo|2=ja|3=神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渡来人|1=toraijin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561926}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other [[:en:Staple (fastener)|kasugai nails]]. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ** Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun|Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.790736111111|N|132.84976388889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|qid=Q11480883}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q65286086]] Wikidata name: Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped Kofun]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped Kofun was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The Kofun features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped Kofuns. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[:en:1941|1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]). * [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57): ** Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. ** Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): ** Diameter: Approx. 34 meters ** Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): ** Width: Approx. 24 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|lt=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|lt=Pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渡来人|1=toraijin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561926}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other [[:en:Staple (fastener)|kasugai nails]]. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[:en:1941|1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]). * [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57): ** Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. ** Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): ** Diameter: Approx. 34 meters ** Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): ** Width: Approx. 24 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|lt=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|lt=Pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** ** The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|lt=wide image-band deity and beast mirror|1=Shinju-kyo|2=ja|3=神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渡来人|1=toraijin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561926}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other [[:en:Staple (fastener)|kasugai nails]]. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ** Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun|Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped Kofun]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped Kofun was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. The Kofun features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped Kofuns. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The burial facilities consist of two [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. [[sn:commons:Category:Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.790736111111|N|132.84976388889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|qid=Q11480883}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q65286086]] Wikidata name: Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped Kofun]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped Kofun was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The Kofun features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped Kofuns. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[:en:1941|1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]). * [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57): ** Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. ** Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): ** Diameter: Approx. 34 meters ** Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): ** Width: Approx. 24 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|lt=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|lt=Pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** ** The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|lt=wide image-band deity and beast mirror|1=Shinju-kyo|2=ja|3=神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraijin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渡来人|1=toraijin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561926}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other [[:en:Staple (fastener)|kasugai nails]]. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ** Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun|Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ** Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun|Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.790736111111|N|132.84976388889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65286086]] Wikidata name: Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped Kofun]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped Kofun was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The Kofun features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped Kofuns. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[:en:1941|1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]). * [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57): ** Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. ** Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): ** Diameter: Approx. 34 meters ** Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): ** Width: Approx. 24 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|lt=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|lt=Pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** ** The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|lt=wide image-band deity and beast mirror|1=Shinju-kyo|2=ja|3=神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渡来人|1=toraijin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561926}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other [[:en:Staple (fastener)|kasugai nails]]. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ** Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun|Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渡来人|1=toraijin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561926}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other [[:en:Staple (fastener)|kasugai nails]]. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ** Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun|Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.790736111111|N|132.84976388889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65286086]] Wikidata name: Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped Kofun]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped Kofun was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The Kofun features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped Kofuns. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[:en:1941|1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]). * [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57): ** Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. ** Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): ** Diameter: Approx. 34 meters ** Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): ** Width: Approx. 24 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|lt=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|lt=Pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** ** The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|lt=wide image-band deity and beast mirror|1=Shinju-kyo|2=ja|3=神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渡来人|1=toraijin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561926}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other [[:en:Staple (fastener)|kasugai nails]]. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ** Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun|Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[:en:1941|1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]). * [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57): ** Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. ** Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): ** Diameter: Approx. 34 meters ** Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): ** Width: Approx. 24 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|lt=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|lt=Pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** ** The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|lt=wide image-band deity and beast mirror|1=Shinju-kyo|2=ja|3=神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渡来人|1=toraijin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561926}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other [[:en:Staple (fastener)|kasugai nails]]. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ** Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun|Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.790736111111|N|132.84976388889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65286086]] Wikidata name: Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped Kofun]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped Kofun was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The Kofun features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped Kofuns. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[:en:1941|1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]). * [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57): ** Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. ** Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): ** Diameter: Approx. 34 meters ** Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): ** Width: Approx. 24 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|lt=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|lt=Pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** ** The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|lt=wide image-band deity and beast mirror|1=Shinju-kyo|2=ja|3=神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渡来人|1=toraijin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561926}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other [[:en:Staple (fastener)|kasugai nails]]. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[:en:1941|1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]). * [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57): ** Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. ** Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): ** Diameter: Approx. 34 meters ** Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): ** Width: Approx. 24 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|lt=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|lt=Pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** ** The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|lt=wide image-band deity and beast mirror|1=Shinju-kyo|2=ja|3=神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渡来人|1=toraijin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561926}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other [[:en:Staple (fastener)|kasugai nails]]. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ** Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun|Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]

The Kofun features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped Kofuns. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** ** The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. [[commons:Category:Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] [[commons:Category:Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun]][[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.790736111111|N|132.84976388889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65286086]] Wikidata name: Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun''' (酒屋高塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in Nishisakaya Town, [[:en:Miyoshi,_Hiroshima|Miyoshi City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Its shape is classified as a [[scallop-shaped Kofun]] (帆立貝形古墳), and it has been designated as a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == This large scallop-shaped Kofun was constructed at the tip of a hill on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]] in northern Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound has suffered significant damage due to erosion and a 1941 ([[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 16) robbery incident. Excavations conducted in [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57) revealed many details about its structure and artifacts. The Kofun features a truncated front projection (square portion) typical of scallop-shaped Kofuns. The circular section (rear mound) is a two-tiered structure. The outer surface of the mound is decorated with stonework, and cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figures), including morning-glory-shaped types, were identified. The surface decorations exhibit techniques unique to the Kibi region, indicating cultural exchanges. The burial facilities consist of two [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|Pit-style stone chambers]] in the rear mound. These chambers show influences from continental craftsmanship. Artifacts discovered include a patterned [[god-beast mirror]] (画文帯神獣鏡), iron tools, magatama beads, and glass beads. The god-beast mirror, a rare artifact with only about 20 similar examples found nationwide, suggests a connection to the Yamato royal authority. The mound's construction is estimated to date to the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the [[Kofun period]]. The Miyoshi area, known for its cluster of scallop-shaped Kofuns, serves as a significant region for exploring relations with the Yamato royal court, the Kibi region, and the Korean Peninsula. The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site in 1982. == History == * [[:en:1941|1941]] (Shōwa 16): Robbery of the rear mound stone chamber, with artifacts discovered (now housed at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]). * [[:en:1982|1982]] (Shōwa 57): ** Excavations conducted by the Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education.. ** Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site on October 14.. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Mound Length: Approx. 46 meters * Circular Section (Rear Mound): ** Diameter: Approx. 34 meters ** Height: 7 meters * Square Section (Front Mound): ** Width: Approx. 24 meters ** Height: 2.5 meters == Burial Facilities == [[File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Relocated Second Burial Chamber}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}.}}]] [[File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Excavated Artifacts}}{{small|Displayed at the {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}}.}}]] As burial facilities, two {{ill|Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|lt=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|lt=Pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (designated as the first and second burial chambers) were constructed within the round mound. The details are as follows: * **First Burial Chamber (First Stone Chamber)** ** The chamber suffered extensive destruction during a robbery in 1941 (Shōwa 16).{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} The chamber is estimated to have measured 3.6 meters in length, 0.83 meters in width, and 1.2 meters in height. Excavated burial items include a {{ill|Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja|画文帯神獣鏡|lt=wide image-band deity and beast mirror|1=Shinju-kyo|2=ja|3=神獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mirror with designs of deities and beasts|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron hoes, iron knives, and iron nails (currently housed at [[:en:Kyoto University|Kyoto University]]). The mirror, considered an imported Chinese product, is particularly significant. Similar mirrors have been unearthed in more than 20 kofun across Japan, suggesting their distribution by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|畿内ヤマト王権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * **Second Burial Chamber (Second Stone Chamber)** ** Discovered during an investigation in 1982 (Shōwa 57),{{Sfn|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|1989}} the chamber was later relocated and reconstructed within the grounds of {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}}. The chamber measures 2.72 meters in length, 0.7–0.84 meters in width, and 0.5 meters in height. Excavated burial items include iron swords, iron nails, magatama beads, and small glass beads. The use of materials such as stone and clay in the construction of these chambers is noted to exhibit {{ill|Toraijin|ja|渡来系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraijin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 渡来人|1=toraijin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561926}} influences. Additionally, the iron nails, used for securing wooden coffins, are identified as artifacts tracing their origins to the Korean Peninsula, as with other [[:en:Staple (fastener)|kasugai nails]]. Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ** Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun|Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]
{{nihongo2|[[:ja:酒屋高塚古墳|酒屋高塚古墳]]}}
''Sakayatakatsuka kofun'' || [[Miyoshi, Hiroshima|Miy ... 71 KB (8,196 words) - 18:55, 10 January {{translated page|ja|酒屋高塚古墳|version=104540834|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:酒屋高塚古墳]] [[ja:%E9%85%92%E5%B1%8B%E9%AB%98%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[Category:Municipal History of Miyoshi]] [[Category:Kofun of Hiroshima prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Hiroshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sakeya Takatsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q65286086}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-B-2.jpg|Overhead view of the relocated second burial chamber Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2, 3D-N-2.jpg|Expanded diagram of the relocated second burial chamber == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ** Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun: Designated on October 14, 1982 (area: 2,159 m²).. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - The second burial chamber of Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun has been relocated and reconstructed here. * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|ja|広島県立みよし風土記の丘|lt=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum|qq=}} (Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City) - Excavated artifacts from Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun are preserved and displayed here. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun}} 9 KB (997 words) - 21:57, 15 January List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima) | align="center"|[[Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun|Sakayatakatsuka Kofun]]
